title: ContinuityCase
description: A candidate self submitted for continuity stress-testing. S = C · P · O · B · K · N. Evaluates whether an inhabited ownership-stream is preserved, branched, or broken.

description.description: What self or continuity candidate is under test.

continuity_components.description: S = C · P · O · B · K · N. Each 0.0–1.0 or null. If any non-null is 0.0, S collapses for that dimension.

C.description: Consciousness / fielded appearance base. From the FieldCase C score or direct estimate.

P.description: Perspective / from-here. Is there a stable point-of-view that carries forward?

O.description: Ownership / mineness. Are experiences given as mine for the same stream?

B.description: Self-boundary. Does a coherent self/non-self organization persist?

K.description: Carried constraint. Do past states remain active as constraints on future states — not merely remembered but causally operative?

N.description: Non-branching continuity. Is there exactly one heir to the current ownership-stream? 0 = stream has branched or been destroyed. 1 = singular continuation.

s_score.description: Computed S score (C·P·O·B·K·N). null if any component is null.

temporal_topology.description: How the ownership-stream behaves over the transformation or interval being tested.

causal_lineage.description: Is the later state causally produced by the prior state (not merely copied from a description)? Required for continuity per the theory.

verdict.description: Continuity verdict. branching_descendants = legitimate heirs but no singular numerically identical continuation.

revision_pressure.description: What this case puts pressure on in the self-continuity model.

breaks_preserved.description: Breaks this case introduced that must not be patched.
